value.name: Bank-Customer-Churn-Prediction-ANN
value.description: Developed an Artificial Neural Network (ANN) model using TensorFlow and Keras to predict bank customer churn with 85–86% accuracy. The project includes data preprocessing, feature engineering, feature scaling, model training, and binary classification.

value.name: Best-Movies-Recommended-From-Every-Genre-
value.description: A Python-based movie recommendation project that analyzes movie genres and ratings to identify popular genres, evaluate genre-wise performance, and recommend the highest-rated movies based on user-selected genres. Built using Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, and Seaborn for data analysis and visualization

value.name: CNN---Cat-vs-Dog-Classification-
value.description: Developed a Deep Learning model using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to classify images as either Cats or Dogs. The model was trained on a labeled image dataset using TensorFlow and Keras. Image preprocessing techniques such as resizing, normalization, and data augmentation were applied to improve model performance and generalization.

value.name: handled-missing-null-values
value.description: “Handled missing (null) values using multiple techniques such as mean, median, mode imputation, forward fill, backward fill, and row removal to improve data quality and ensure accurate analysis and model performance.”

value.name: HR-Analytics-Dashboard-PowerBI
value.description: Interactive HR Analytics Dashboard built in Power BI to analyze employee demographics, attrition, compensation, performance, and career growth using the IBM HR Analytics dataset.

value.name: KNN---Iris-Flower-Classification
value.description: Developed a Machine Learning model to classify Iris flowers into three species: Setosa, Versicolor, and Virginica based on flower measurements such as sepal length & width, petal length & width. Data visualization techniques were used to analyze feature relationships, and classification algorithms were applied to accurately predict flower species.

value.name: N-Gram-Analysis-in-NLP-Natural-Language-Processing-
value.description: Developed an NLP-based text analysis project to generate and analyze N-Grams from textual data. The project extracts frequently occurring word sequences such as Bigrams, Trigrams, and Four-Grams to identify language patterns, word relationships, and repeated phrases within a text corpus. NLTK libraries were used for tokenization& N-Gram generation.

value.name: One-Hot-Encoding
value.description: Applied One-Hot Encoding to convert categorical features such as Gender, Married, Education, and Self_Employed into numerical format. This created binary columns for each category, making the data suitable for machine learning models and improving prediction accuracy.

value.name: Ordinal-Encoding-using-Python-and-Pandas
value.description: Developed a feature engineering project to convert ordinal categorical data into numerical values using mapping techniques in Python. The project demonstrates how ordered categories such as clothing sizes (S, M, L, XL, XXL) can be transformed into meaningful numerical representations for Machine Learning models.

value.name: panda-numpy
value.description: This project demonstrates data analysis and manipulation using NumPy and Pandas. It includes data cleaning, transformation, and basic insights generation from datasets.

value.name: seaborn-matplotlib
value.description: A data visualization project built using Matplotlib and Seaborn. The project showcases different graphical representations to analyze patterns, trends, and relationships in the dataset.

value.name: Single-Layer-Perceptron-for-Binary-Classification
value.description: Developed a SLP model to perform binary classification using supervised learning. The model learns a linear decision boundary that separates data points into two classes based on input features. Decision region visualization was used to understand how the perceptron classifies different data samples & draws a separating hyperplane between classes.

value.name: Stemming-and-Lemmatization-in-NLP
value.description: Developed an NLP text preprocessing project to compare Stemming & Lemmatization techniques. The project demonstrates how words are reduced to their root or base forms to improve text analysis & machine learning performance. Various stemming algorithms such as Porter, Regexp, Snowball&Lancaster Stemmers were implemented&compared with Lemmatization.

value.name: Stop-Words-Extraction-using-NLTK
value.description: Developed an NLP preprocessing project to identify and extract English stop words using the NLTK library. Stop words are commonly used words such as "a", "an", "the", "is", "are", "and" that generally carry little semantic meaning in text analysis.

value.name: Stop-Words-Removal-using-Natural-Language-Processing-NLP-
value.description: Developed an NLP-based text preprocessing project to identify and remove stop words from textual data. Stop words are commonly occurring words such as "is", "am", "are", "the", "and", "of" that usually do not contribute significant meaning to text analysis. Removing these words helps improve the efficiency and accuracy of NLP and ML models.

value.name: Supply-Chain-Analytics
value.description: End-to-end Supply Chain Analytics project with data cleaning, DAX measures, and interactive Power BI visualizations.

value.name: Word-and-Sentence-Tokenization-using-NLP
value.description: Developed a NLP project to perform word & sentence tokenization on textual data. Tokenization is the process of breaking text into smaller meaningful units such as sentences and words. This preprocessing technique is essential for various NLP tasks, including text analysis, sentiment analysis, machine translation,&chatbot development.

value.name: Word-Lemmatization-using-NLTK-Mice-Mouse-
value.description: Developed an NLP preprocessing project that applies lemmatization to convert inflected words into their meaningful dictionary root forms (lemmas). Unlike stemming, lemmatization considers the context and vocabulary of a word. For example, the plural noun "mice" is correctly transformed into "mouse", preserving the actual meaning of the word.
